THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE RESOLUTION OF SAN MATEO COUNTY CASE 518217. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS RAISING THE ISSUES SET FORTH IN THE REMAINDER OF THE DEMURRER SHOULD THE STAY BE LIFTED AND WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS RAISING THE ISSUES SET FORTH IN THE COMPLAINT IN THE SAN MATEO ACTION. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Continued To Jan-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Michael Myers, Jeffrey Kaiser, William Kaiser And Chris Catteron'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar May-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK RUSHIN, TEL. NO. 1-415-279-5271 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY H. BELOTE OF MORRIS POLICH & PURDY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-984-8508 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Michael Myers, Jeffrey Kaiser, William Kaiser And Chris Catteron'S Demurrer to the sole cause of action for breach of a written contract is sustained on the grounds of another action pending. This case is stayed pending the resolution of San Mateo County case 518217. Plaintiffs have offered no persuasive reason why this case was filed in this county given the pendency of case 518217 and the court cannot discern any. Judicial economy is disserved by the filing of this case in this county. As an alternative ground for the stay, the court exercises its inherent authority to stay this case in favor of a pending earlier filed case in another county. This order is without prejudice to defendants raising the issues set forth in the remainder of the demurrer should the stay be lifted and without prejudice to plaintiffs raising the issues set forth in the complaint in the San Mateo action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter; Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

Defendants Michael Myers, Jeffrey Kaiser, William Kaiser And Chris Catteron'S Motion For Disqualification Of Mark Rushin As Counsel For Loanvest And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Section 128.5 Against South Bay And Mark Rushin
Off Calendar May-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK RUSHIN, TEL. NO. 1-415-279-5271 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY H. BELOTE OF MORRIS POLICH & PURDY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-984-8508 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Michael Myers, Jeffrey Kaiser, William Kaiser And Chris Catteron'S Motion For Disqualification Of Mark Rushin As Counsel For Loanvest And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Section 128.5 Against South Bay And Mark Rushin is Granted and their request for sanctions is denied. By virtue of his concurrent representation of Loanvest XI in this case and representation of parties suing Loanvest XI in the San Mateo case, Mr. Rushin is automatically disqualified. Mr. Rushin's decision to withdraw from his representation of parties suing Loanvest XI in the San Mateo case does not permit him to remain as counsel in this case. Defendants' request for sanctions is denied because there has been no showing that they complied with the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 128.7. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter; Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
